content: We can frame the original task-specific Self-Refine framework as a base instantiation of a general LLM self-reflection mechanism, by extending its iterative self-feedback loop to incorporate multiple inconsistent solving perspectives from modern reflection research to break local optimization traps. This modified framework retains Self-Refine's core advantage of requiring no extra training or supervised data, while addressing the common limitation that single-perspective self-feedback often misses blind spots in the original output.
